In the 1964 film version, it was sung by Bill Shirley, dubbing for actor Jeremy Brett.

samples.question: Men Against the Sea and Pitcairn's Island were two sequels to what famous novel?
samples.answer:
The answer can be inferred from [S2], which states that "Men Against the Sea is the second installment in the trilogy by Charles Nordhoff and James Norman Hall about the mutiny aboard HMS Bounty." This suggests that Men Against the Sea and Pitcairn's Island are sequels to another novel in the same trilogy.

The first installment, Mutiny on the "Bounty", is mentioned in [S2] as well. Therefore, it can be concluded that Men Against the Sea and Pitcairn's Island were two sequels to Mutiny on the "Bounty".

Answer: Mutiny on the "Bounty".

[S1] A Cyclops ( ; ; plural Cyclopes ; ), in Greek mythology and later Roman mythology, was a member of a primordial race of giants, each with a single eye in the middle of his forehead.  The name literally means "round-eyed"  or "circle-eyed".

Hesiod described three one-eyed Cyclopes, Brontes, Steropes and Arges, the sons of Uranus and Gaia, brothers of the Titans, builders and craftsmen,  while the epic poet Homer described another group of mortal herdsmen Cyclopes the sons of Poseidon. Other accounts were wr
---
